text.title: Outsource Onboarding One-Pager Template Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Outsource Onboarding One-Pager Template. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Onboarding doc is exactly one page (≤500 words). Longer = unread.
rule.rationale: Contractor reads once on day 0; one page is the upper bound for full retention.

rule.statement: Six named sections: scope / deliverables / comms cadence / access list / payment terms / kill-switch. Missing any = the gap that bites later.
rule.rationale: Each named section corresponds to a known onboarding failure mode; omitting one invites that failure.

rule.statement: First deliverable named for ≤24 hrs from start (small, scoped). Validates fit + access works.
rule.rationale: Day-1 shipping forces resolution of every access/process issue immediately.

rule.statement: Kill-switch named: trial period (2 weeks), evaluation criterion, exit pay terms. Said upfront, not on day 14.
rule.rationale: Implicit trial = legal mess + bad ending; explicit kill-switch is fair to both sides.

rule.statement: Same template per hire; evolve it post-engagement based on learnings.
rule.rationale: Per-hire bespoke onboarding multiplies effort; template lets the org learn instead of the contractor.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-outsource-onboarding-one-pager-template.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
